story: Pedaling, paddling, and skating his way across continents and oceans, Jason Lewis achieved an incredible feat in 2007. title: Knuckleball King's Incredible Milestone
story: Batter up! Phil Niekro, the New York Yankees' knuckleball wizard, threw his way into baseball history in 1985. title: Ancient Footsteps Reveal Early American History
story: Imagine walking where humans first stepped in America! title: Ancient Ape Ancestor Discovered in Kenya

On October 6, 1948, this famous fossil hunter discovered part of a skull from Proconsul africanus on Rusinga Island, Kenya. title: The World's First Globe Maker
story: A curious boy named Martin Behaim was born in Nuremberg, Germany in 1459. He grew up to become a navigator and geographer who changed how we see the world. Behaim designed the Erdapfel, which means 'earth apple' in German. This special creation wasn't for eating—it was the very first globe of our planet! His invention helped people understand Earth's shape and geography in a whole new way.

bonus_fact: 'Something' became one of The Beatles' most covered songs, with over 150 artists recording their own versions.

title: From Player to Super Bowl Champion Coach
story: Tony Dungy's football journey began when he was born in Jackson, Michigan in 1955. He grew up to become a legendary coach, leading the Indianapolis Colts to victory in Super Bowl XLI. Dungy's brilliant strategies and leadership skills earned him a spot in the Pro Football Hall of Fame. On October 6, we celebrate the birth of this coaching icon.
activity_question: Which team did Tony Dungy lead to a Super Bowl victory?
bonus_fact: Tony Dungy was named NFL Coach of the Year twice, in 1997 and 2005.

title: Batter Up! The First World Series
story: Cheers erupted as the Cincinnati Red Stockings stepped up to the plate. It was 1882, and baseball fans were buzzing with excitement. On October 6, the very first World Series game kicked off at Bank Street Grounds in Cincinnati. The Red Stockings faced off against the Chicago White Stockings, scoring a shutout victory of 4-0. This historic match marked the beginning of a beloved American tradition.
activity_question: Which team won the first World Series game?
bonus_fact: The 'World Series' name wasn't used until years later. This early championship was called the 'Championship of the United States.'
